items.title: Buyers 'back in the driver's seat as property market slows in south-east Queensland
items.summary: After a decades-long real estate boom, experts say the balance of power is finally shifting from sellers to buyers.

items.title: Live: US, Iran exchange fire as Trump issues new threats and blockade starts
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says he will not charge a 20 per cent toll on traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, as the US military confirms its naval blockade of Iran has come into effect. Follow live.

items.title: Telstra compensation process puts onus on customers, consumer body says
items.summary: Telstra is assessing outage compensation claims, but consumer advocates say the process is cumbersome and puts the onus on customers rather than the telco at fault.

items.title: As swift parrots kept breeding in this forest, the government realised it had a problem
items.summary: It was late 2021 when one of Australia's rarest birds started to arrive in the native forests of Lonnavale. Ecologists had expected it but for a reacting forestry sector, it was "not sustainable".
